explanation: This command adds quotes around the executable name to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to such insertion. It uses the exact flags from the rule and is a valid test case.
This command omits quotes and extra spaces, testing if the Sigma rule is sensitive to minimal formatting.
This command reorders the flags to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to argument order.
This command substitutes '--silent' with '-silent' to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to short/long option syntax.
This command appends an extra '--' to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to additional arguments or separators.
This command appends an escape character (`) to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to trailing escape characters.
This command adds a trailing space to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to trailing whitespace.
This command appends a caret (^) to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to escape characters in the command line.
This command appends a quote to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to trailing quotes.
This command appends a space within quotes to test if the Sigma rule is sensitive to embedded whitespace in quoted strings.
